item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this would end with some kind of implication of what actually happened, but I'm glad it didn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If I were the Council, I would assign SCP-3557 the Object Class Keter. Here's why.

The main issue here is that they don't know what the Object IS, and they will not reach a consensus any time soon. However, in my headcanon, there is protocol for just this event.

In the event that there is any unknown Anomalous Object, interactions and transportation are to be conducted with the assumption that the object is Provisionally Keter, in addition to all memetic and mental precautions. Since the object in this case is unknown, with an unknown threat level and an unknown range of effect, the Provisional Keter status certainly applies here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something about this sends a chill down my spine, like the fact that it was detectable but nothing happened and no one knows what caused it.
In my opinion the most probable scenario is that some super advanced alien civilization did an experiment that went terribly wrong, causing the universe to be physically changed forever. In a lot of ways this reminds me of the OMG Particle, but on a much greater scale, and the Oh My God Particle already kinda freaks me out to begin with. One of the best pieces of cosmic horror I've seen here in awhile. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something weird happening with time was something I considered, but I decided to just not to go with that angle. I had briefly considered that 3557 was the result of the time rewinding from a point in the future, but decided to drop because this was already getting complex enough as is. That said, if I were to include that hypothesis I would also have evidence against it in Yolas's letter. I'd say something along the lines of the Foundation having seen other time-fucky stuff before, and this didn't set off any of their "temporal anomaly alarms".

Yolas is Clearance Level 5, and the document in this article is written at that level. My headcanon is that Department/Division heads are all Level 5 personnel, because they basically have to be. If say, an anomaly under the purview of the Linguistics Department had something restricted to Level 5 Clearance, it wouldn't make sense for nobody in the Linguistics Department to have access. It also doesn't make sense for all Level 5 information to be in the hands of thirteen people - that'd put way too much on their plates. Since Yolas is the head of Ontokinetics Division, she is Level 5, at least with regard to anything to do with ontokinetics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Congratulations on the +100! I really love how this turns the usual format on its head: instead of the Foundation being baffled because something weird happened, they're baffled because something weird didn't happen. And they're still freaked out about it.

If the rate of time isn't consistent across dimensions, one possibility is a CK-scenario occurred and drastically sped up the relative passage of time, then was cleanly reverted by the Foundation "within" it (possibly over the course of CK-weeks/months/years) before off-world multi-U personnel could investigate. It's a variant on the Rebound theory, though with a more credible explanation than "There's a natural state the universe wants to be in".

Also god damn Dr. Yolas knows a lot of Foundation secrets, she's practically an O5 with that level of clearance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That would also be an CK-Class Scenario, and there isn't evidence to support it.

Additionally, all of the devices the Foundation uses to measure in from the external stuff also measure out, simply by nature of how they operate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if internal reality didn't change, but external reality changed? Nothing changed on Earth, but their way of measuring changes with picket dimensions and stuff did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very late response, but I had to respond to this. It stayed on my mind for a while.

A) This would not have worked as a tale. A large part of this relies on the progression of the SCP, which begins the reader with something that is not the standard format. This throws the reader off, and then makes them think something is wrong. This is the feeling I wanted, and would not be present in a tale.

This also deals, in large part, with a singular anomaly in a clinical way. That is an SCP to me, and not a tale. A tale is prose, and this is not. Sure, this deviates significantly from the traditional format, but there it still focuses on an SCP. This could have easily been adapted to follow a normal SCP format, but it would not have been nearly as compelling.

B) If you dislike this because it uses Humes, I really question how much you actually read into it. This is not a usage of Humes as an explanation. In fact, the inclusion of Humes in this SCP is anything but. The Foundation's expectation that Humes explain things only serves to heighten their confusion and make things even more baffling.

For all the Humes and SRAs and magitech the Foundation had, none of that let them know what happened at 0427 UTC, 18/03/2016. This is a rebuttal of Humes, a refuttation of an all knowing Foundation. This takes the "Foundation that has mastered the anomaly and knows this cause" and places them in a situation of unknown - the usage of Humes heightens the sense of the unknown here, completely contrary to the usage you probably dislike.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An event so beyond the Foundation's power level they aren't even sure it happened. Beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Humes don't have anything to them except a measurement of reality, though. It's not an explanation of reality bending, it's just a complex ruler the Foundation uses to quantify it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: a tale masquerading as an SCP, and also i have said it before and i'll say it again, humes are the midichlorians of the SCP stories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I may add on, as part of my framing in my 001 proposal, said proposal was framed as something that is about to change its own SCP number. The basis for that plot point is that the 001 slot (and practically every other slot) are interchangeable variables, to be filled in by any anomaly freely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If 3557 actually did create a new reality, the new reality would not be any more or less inherently anomalous than what came before it. As such, there wouldn't be any cause for them to die off in the new reality.

2608 can survive in baseline reality after 3557. What does that mean? Well, it could mean nothing happened, it could mean that reality snapped back to normal, or it could mean that there's no anomalous indicators normally present in the new, changed reality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this! It has a uniquely mundane feel for something concerning a CK, which is something I feel was missing from the site

I have to ask, though; wouldn't checking whether or not 2608 can survive on earth again either rule out or confirm the "exactly the same as before" theory?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've replaced all the instances of hypothesis in the article with theory.

My problem with this is one of my major problems with SCP-2798, and that would be that it assumed there was an SCP-001 to begin with. Yes, I know, different canons and what not and I know I can headcanon that part out, but it's just a tad bit harder to do.

I'm not sure what you're getting at here, but I think it's that you're objecting to the crosslinking to a 001 proposal?

I understand how some people might not like that, and that's personal preference. My personal headcanon is that there are multiple 001s, and that's evident in this article. (There are crosslinks to two 001s in the article - Wrong and spikebrennan). I'm not trying to hold up any 001 as "canon" but rather I just included some I thought worked. I wanted another reality anchored site, Facility T made sense. There are noticeable similarities between this and Wrong Proposal, so I felt a crosslink would make sense. (In fact, this has now been crosslinked on the Wrong Proposal by Mr. Wrong)

If you don't like the 001 crosslinks, then you can just ignore them or treat them differently. The one CK they've seen doesn't have to be the Wrong Proposal, and Facility T doesn't have to be God's Blind Spot. They're not major parts of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's something fishy…but I can't quite put my finger on it. As for my problems with this:

The first event is incredibly unlikely, but entirely possible. Our knowledge of CK-Class events has major holes and gaps - we've only seen one total CK-Class shift, and everything else has been small.

My problem with this is one of my major problems with SCP-2798, and that would be that it assumed there was an SCP-001 to begin with. Yes, I know, different canons and what not and I know I can headcanon that part out, but it's just a tad bit harder to do.

Secondly, it's the use of the word ''hypothesis''. I'm pretty sure you're all smart enough to know what that means, so I'm not going to explain it. Yes, the Foundation might have the equipment needed to measure what the hell went on with the SCP itself, but they don't seem to be getting that many results.

I know I said I wouldn't explain what the word meant, but a hypothesis is a statement that can be tested. If the Foundation doesn't even know how to test it, why are they using the term hypothesis? I'm probably going to be corrected on this, so don't be afraid to offend me.

That's all.

Sorry for my lack of a format, I haven't been using the comment section here in a long while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Extranormal Event: SCP-3557 is one and done with - there's nothing to contain.

Reminds me of my 001 proposal… and then it was actually crosslinked. Glad I am not alone in my thought.

While my 001 looked at the Foundation itself being a reaction to a CK-class event, this article shows that the Foundation itself is still not enough to handle a CK-class event.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edited my comment while you did this, there's more to be looked at.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've gone and proofread it, there were a bunch of small errors I needed to correct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The soft ending is somewhat intentional - it's not meant to give a hard conclusion to what happened, because the Foundation doesn't have a solid conclusion themselves.

I've gone over and made proofreading changes.

item.title: Re: CK or OK?
item.content:encoded: Oh shoot, I missed that. I'll wait for the tagging team to get this then; they already removed the tags.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This certainly should have been proofread. It just looks sloppy with the amount of errors it has1, which destroyed immersion to the point where I did not feel it worth my time to continue reading.

ETR: I'm not awake enough, that was fine, but 'Restructing' in several places was distracting enough on its own.

Clarifying because I totally pointed out something that I misread, so I feel as though I should make up for that some:

+ show block
– hide block

These new devices are to utilize a wider spectrum of mechanisms in order to detect any potential changes to reality, which testing involving localized CK-Class Scenarios testing the effectiveness of these techniques.

I cannot parse the latter half of this. I think you mean to say something like:

These new devices are to utilize a wider spectrum of mechanisms in order to detect any potential changes to reality. Localized CK-Class Scenarios will be induced by personnel in order to test the effectiveness of these techniques.

Once all Mark II CSDs have been constructed and tested in rigorous settings, they are to be installed in any and all sites that meet the Reality Integrity Division's guidelines on a reality-anchored location.

Testing doesn't automagically mean they work, so it makes no sense to use them just because they've put them 'in rigorous settings'. This is also a bit redundant from what just came before, so I think it just needs to be rewritten to more seamlessly follow from the previous sentence.

In general, this is any site/area/LoI that is either located in a location with an abnormally high Hume level, artificially reality anchored, or located in an extradimensional location, even those we don't have sites in.

This makes little semantic sense in context, and is a bit of a mess to the point where I couldn't find the proper way to restructure it, so you can thank The Great Hippo for this suggestion:

This is any site, area, or LoI that is either extradimensional or located in a region with an abnormally high Hume level.

You don't need to state 'in general' you're already giving the breakdown, you don't need the last bit because you already mention 'any area' that fits these parameters, which already includes places that may not have a site.

extradimensionally located or reality anchored Foundation site.

Should these be hyphenated?

The only evidence for SCP-3557's occurrence is the simultaneous activation of all CSDs (CK-Class Scenario Detectors) located at every extradimensionally located or reality anchored Foundation site.6 At 0427 UTC, 18/03/2016, each extradimensional or reality anchored site sent an automated message to all Foundation sites denoting that a CK-Class Scenario occurred

The second sentence is incredibly verbose for how little information it actually imparts.

I'll cut it off here.

TLDR small SPaG issues aside, there are quite a few places where it's too verbose and technical for its own good, resulting in unwieldy phrasing and sentence construction. Format-screws are cool and all, but the writing needs to be good enough to support it, and shouldn't be overlooked to this degree.

Footnotes
1. Several of which I fixed, before I realized how many small issues there were

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

5. Dr. Leslie Yolas is looking for a lot more funding.

This was a great read. I really like how technical it is. My favorite part is this:

1) Synced pseudo-random number generators located in baseline and extradimensional; designed to generate numbers in the same fashion, and thus, provide the same numbers
2) Kant and Geiger counters physically located in extradimensional/anchored reality but monitoring baseline reality
3) Atomic clocks synced to one another
4) Regular database cross references, functioning by hashing random sections of the database and comparing hashes to each other
5) Applied thaumaturgical sensors set up to alarm if a large change in the state of reality is detected
6) Electromagnetical sensors detecting any sudden changes in background elecromagnetic radiation patterns in baseline

All of these except 2a and 5 are entirely possible IRL and would actually make sense to do if one truly thought reality had a chance to change. Very clever! You put a lot of thought into this and it shows. There's also a lot of crosslinking porn, and while I'm not always a fan of that, it works very well in this context.

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Tags cleared, since there was at least one missing. If you're not familiar with all of the tags used by the site, please let one of our experienced taggers add the tags.

item.title: Re: CK or OK?
item.content:encoded:
Articles do not count until they have been tagged by the Tagging Team. No self-tagging allowed, especially if you're planning on leaving a tag out on purpose

…Did you miss that bit?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs a bit of an edit sweep (for instance, it happened at the time, not on the time), and it's got a very soft ending (I was waiting for a whammy), but I love the overwhelming sense of "Did something happened, what was it, would we even know if it had, and why don't we?" It's like what I was trying to do with 2832, and I love anything that paints the Foundation as not the all-knowing jailors they like to think they are. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely loved this. I adore articles that aren’t afraid to dive into the hardboiled technical lore this site, and this is a perfect example of that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Also: our database cross checkers were only set up to register if there were discrepancies and where they were, but not what those discrepancies actually were. The idea was that we'd have the data to actually crosscheck.

Missing ) here.

item.title: CK or OK?
item.content:encoded:
"Did the world end that morning?"
"I'm not really sure."

First format screw, although there is a perfectly normal SCP buried in there.

At one point, this was intended as an entry for the 2015 Tag-Less Challenge. It only gets 10 points there, because it does have three additional tags, but the object class tag is unclassed to make up for that. This wasn't originally made for that, but then I found out about it and decided to shift it up to make it work. (If I really wanted, I probably could have made this a generic warping event, not necessarily a CK)

The one big thing I had to change was a list of GoI responses I had included at one point. I've included them below (as a note, they're all meant to be canon to the article). Now that it's gotten tagged with a couple tags, I could add these back, but I think the article works better the way it is right now.

+ GoI Responses
– hide block

Other GoI's that monitor the integrity of reality have been reached as to whether or not they also experienced a similar event at 0427 UTC, 18/03/2016. All that have responded have indicated their detectors also malfunctioned:1

  • Global Occult Coalition: "… while we will not elaborate on the methods we used to determine if such an event took place, we can confirm that we detected an event such as you have described …"
  • Unidentified Incidents Unit: "… PatternSweep detected Positive at 11:27 EST 03/17/2016, and the matter is currently under active investigation … "
  • Marshall, Carter and Dark: "… Every single one of our anomalous tripwires was malfunctioned at 0427 UTC, 18/03/2016. This constitutes a massive loss of profit, exceeding $5,000,000…"
  • ORIA: "… We have reason to believe that the events of 7:57 AM, 18 March 2016 represent widespread anomalous influence of unknown…"
  • Horizon Initiative: "…several of our artifacts detected a notable shift in Hume and Akiva levels at the indicated time…"2
  • Swiss Government: "… our anomaly-detection system has indicated that 0527 Zurich Time should have a rating of Leuthari II/Mederich, indicating an absolute certainty of anomaly…"

Thanks to Leveritas and DrChandra for looking this over in draft form.

If you liked this thing and would like to read some comments about it, or check out other things I've written, head to my author page for more!

Footnotes
1. These aren't the full responses, only the relevant excerpts. - Dr. Yolas
2. Ontokinetics hadn't thought it was indicative of CK-Class events, but this calls that into question. - Dr. Yolas
